# /home/michael

> A framework for the entire knowledge creation pipeline: from identifying problems worth solving, to learning systematically, to sharing what you've learned in ways that matter.

This site is maintained by Michael Rowe, an academic and researcher working on technology-enhanced scholarship and artificial intelligence in health professions education at the University of Lincoln.

## What this site offers

- **AI Literacy Framework**: A comprehensive approach to developing AI literacy across six dimensions (access/recognition, critical evaluation, functional application, creation/communication, ethical awareness, contextual judgement)
- **AI Literacy Course**: A developmental course for academics moving from foundation through substitution, adaptation, to transformation
- **Essays and Analysis**: Scholarly writing on AI in education, learning alignment, taste and judgement in human-AI systems, institutional AI governance, and documentation as infrastructure
- **Agentic workflow series**: Three posts on what changes when AI agents handle execution and humans operate at the direction layer — covering individual practice, prerequisites for effective delegation, and institutional governance
- **Frameworks and Policies**: Practical resources including classroom AI policies and curriculum design frameworks
- **Concept notes**: Atomic definitions of key ideas including agentic workflows, harness engineering, context engineering, vibe coding, and AI agents

## Primary audience

Health professions educators and those involved in developing practitioners at any level, including medical educators, clinical supervisors, curriculum designers, and faculty development professionals.

## Key resources

- /Courses/AI-literacy/ - Full AI literacy course for academics
- /Frameworks/AI-literacy-development-framework/ - Framework for embedding AI literacy into existing courses
- /Policies/classroom-policy-AI-use/ - Template classroom policy for AI use
- /Notes/AI-literacy/ - Core concepts of AI literacy
- /Essays/taste-and-judgement/ - Essay on taste and judgement in human-AI systems
- /Essays/documentation-as-infrastructure/ - Essay on documentation as the infrastructure that makes agentic delegation coherent
- /Essays/institutional-ai-governance/ - Essay on AI governance in higher education institutions
- /posts/ai-agents-academic-workflow/ - Post 1: what agentic workflows look like in academic practice
- /posts/working-effectively-with-ai-agents/ - Post 2: three prerequisites for effective agent-first work (plan, documentation as infrastructure, domain expertise)
- /posts/ai-agent-governance-higher-education/ - Post 3: what AI agent governance requires at institutional scale; the harness concept
- /Notes/agentic-workflows/ - Concept note: agentic workflows
- /Notes/harness-engineering/ - Concept note: harness engineering (the architecture that makes delegation coherent)
- /Notes/context-engineering/ - Concept note: context engineering
- /Notes/vibe-coding/ - Concept note: vibe coding and its disciplined alternative

## Content structure

- **Essays**: Developed arguments and analysis
- **Posts**: Shorter, timely pieces
- **Notes**: Atomic concepts and working ideas
- **Courses**: Structured learning sequences
- **Frameworks**: Practical tools for educators

## Licence and attribution

Content is shared openly. Attribution appreciated. Academic citation format:
Rowe, M. (2026). [Title]. /home/michael. https://michael-rowe.github.io/home-michael/

## Contact

For questions or collaboration: https://michael-rowe.github.io/home-michael/contact
